info.title: crucible
info.description: REST API for the crucible daemon, a Firecracker microVM sandbox runtime. The daemon is the contract every SDK mirrors. Auth is a bearer token (`Authorization: Bearer <key>`); `/healthz` is always exempt, and a loopback daemon with no keys configured serves unauthenticated. The exec and service-log endpoints stream a binary frame protocol OpenAPI cannot model — see the guides for the frame format.

post.description: Creates a named app the daemon keeps a healthy instance of, re-creating it from spec after a daemon restart. desired_state defaults to "running". The instance boots asynchronously; the response is the app's initial state.

delete.description: Removes the app and tears down its instance on the next reconcile.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

schema.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

get.description: Desired state plus observed status (instance id, phase, health, restarts).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

schema.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

put.description: Replaces the app's spec (name immutable) and redeploys its instance from the new spec — the daemon bumps the app's generation and the reconciler destroys the old instance and boots a fresh one. Desired running/stopped is retained.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

schema.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

get.description: WebSocket interactive exec against the app's current instance; identical contract to GET /sandboxes/{id}/exec. 409 when the app has no running instance.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

schema.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

post.summary: Run a command in an app's current instance (streams frames)
post.description: Resolves the app to its current instance and runs the command there, streaming the same length-prefixed frame protocol as POST /sandboxes/{id}/exec (?stdin=1 for a hijacked interactive session). Resolution is per-request, so it targets whatever instance is current across a self-heal or rolling update. 409 when the app has no running instance.

parameters.name: stdin
parameters.description: Set to "1" for an interactive, full-duplex exec (hijacked stream).

schema.description: Set to "1" for an interactive, full-duplex exec (hijacked stream).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

schema.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

get.description: Durable logs (service output + exec activity) of the app's current instance; same shape as GET /sandboxes/{id}/logs. 409 when the app has no running instance, 501 with no log store.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Byte offset to read from; -1 (default) tails the recent log.

schema.description: Byte offset to read from; -1 (default) tails the recent log.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Filter by source: service | exec | all (default all).

schema.description: Filter by source: service | exec | all (default all).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

schema.description: App name (a DNS label, e.g. web).

get.description: Always returns 200 and is exempt from auth, so probes work without a key.

post.summary: Pull and convert an OCI image
post.description: Experimental. Pulls a registry image and converts it to a bootable rootfs. Returns 501 when image support is not enabled (set --image-dir).
Experimental. Imports an image from a docker-save tar on the request body (application/octet-stream); ?tag selects one image in a multi-image archive.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Which image inside a multi-image docker-save archive to import.

schema.description: Which image inside a multi-image docker-save archive to import.

get.description: The rootfs profiles the daemon can boot sandboxes from.
Lists stored registry credentials as host + username only — never the secret.

post.description: Adds or replaces the credential used to pull from a private registry (`crucible registry login`). The secret is write-only — it is never returned by any endpoint. 501 when no credential store is configured.

delete.description: Deletes the stored credential for a registry host (`crucible registry logout`).

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: Registry host, e.g. ghcr.io or index.docker.io.

schema.description: Registry host, e.g. ghcr.io or index.docker.io.

post.description: Boots a Firecracker microVM. All fields are optional; an empty body uses the daemon defaults. `image` boots from a converted OCI image (pulled on demand — see the images endpoints); a daemon without an image store answers 501.

get.description: WebSocket upgrade for a full-duplex interactive exec. The client's first message is the JSON ExecRequest; after that, the concatenated binary message payloads in each direction form exactly the same length-prefixed frame stream as POST ?stdin=1 (stdin/stdin_close up, stdout/stderr/exit down). Frames may split across messages — decode the concatenated stream. Non-WebSocket GETs answer 426.

post.description: Runs a command in the guest and streams the result as length-prefixed frames ([type:1][reserved:3][size:4 BE][payload]) where type is stdout, stderr, or exit; the exit payload is a JSON ExecResult. Set ?stdin=1 for a hijacked, full-duplex interactive exec.

parameters.name: stdin
parameters.description: Set to "1" for an interactive, full-duplex exec (hijacked stream).

schema.description: Set to "1" for an interactive, full-duplex exec (hijacked stream).

get.description: Returns the raw bytes of one guest file (?path), capped at ?max_bytes. Content only, guest → host; nothing is written host-side.

post.description: Extracts a tar stream (the request body, application/octet-stream) beneath ?path in the guest filesystem; the `crucible cp` push path. Rejects entries that escape the destination.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Guest destination directory the tar is extracted beneath.

schema.description: Guest destination directory the tar is extracted beneath.

get.description: Durable per-sandbox logs (service output + exec activity) that survive the sandbox. Returns 501 when the daemon has no log store configured.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Byte offset to read from; -1 (default) tails the recent log.

schema.description: Byte offset to read from; -1 (default) tails the recent log.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Filter by source: service | exec | all (default all).

schema.description: Filter by source: service | exec | all (default all).

put.description: Experimental. Sets and (re)starts the guest's supervised long-lived entrypoint.

get.description: Experimental. Streams the supervised service's stdout/stderr as it is produced.

post.description: Creates count sandboxes from the snapshot (query param or body; body wins). All-or-nothing: a mid-fork failure rolls back. The optional body's publish maps host ports onto the fork (docker -p semantics); publish requires count 1 because host ports are exclusive.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of sandboxes to fork (default 1). The body's count wins when both are set.

schema.description: Number of sandboxes to fork (default 1). The body's count wins when both are set.

get.description: Reports whether the token is scoped and, if so, the policy the daemon enforces for it.

bearerAuth.description: Daemon API key. Omit on a keyless loopback daemon.
